top_priorities_ordered.title: Industrial-Grade Documentation Matrix & Zero-Friction Onboarding
top_priorities_ordered.description: Activated highest-level documentation and product delivery protocol based on Diátaxis framework. Completely rewrote the project's front page (README.md) with a one-sentence pitch, core feature matrix, and zero-friction quick start. Authored the Creator's Whitepaper (USER_GUIDE.md) detailing natural language intent translation, blueprint derivation, and the interactive animatic REPL. Created a Troubleshooting & Self-Rescue Manual (TROUBLESHOOTING.md) to guide users through blackbox log tracing, Defender whitelist configuration, and garbage collection. The documentation strictly adheres to the current codebase without any hallucinations.

top_priorities_ordered.title: Aviation-Grade Blackbox, In-Flight Garbage Collection & Magic Number Elimination
top_priorities_ordered.description: Implemented system-wide purge and observability: (1) Aviation-grade blackbox flight recorder capturing unhandled exceptions via sys.excepthook with double-fault protection; (2) Two-level intelligent garbage collection (startup cold sweep + in-flight hot pruning) gated by temporal safety checks; (3) Centralized Settings module eliminating scattered magic numbers with full env-var override capability; (4) Lightweight lazy-import guards for heavy ML dependencies (Gymnasium, Numba, Optuna).

top_priorities_ordered.title: Semantic Translation, Interactive Preview REPL & Blueprint Controlled Evolution
top_priorities_ordered.description: Implemented four-pillar Director Studio: (1) Semantic-to-Parametric Translation mapping fuzzy vibe strings to strongly-typed CreatorIntentSpec; (2) Interactive Animatic REPL with sub-second proxy preview and multi-round feedback; (3) Blueprint Asset Lineage with pure YAML serialization and backward-compatible deserialization; (4) Controlled Variational Evolution with sacred freeze-mask enforcement ensuring zero variance on locked gene families.

trigger_when: The user explicitly asks for precise, parallel, global, or comprehensive research
A subsystem upgrade is blocked by missing external knowledge

explicit_trigger_phrases.hard_trigger: 本轮禁止直接开做，必须先启动精准并行研究协议

research_trigger_policy.deep_reading_trigger_rule: When the user names a specific paper, repo, or documentation as the primary reference (e.g., 'this paper is your guiding light'), MUST switch from parallel scanning to Deep Reading Protocol. Parallel discovery finds WHAT to build; Deep Reading finds HOW to build it. Never skip Deep Reading for North Star sources.
research_trigger_policy.phrase_interpretation_rule: Close paraphrases should count as valid triggers when the intent is clearly precise, parallel, dedup-aware, implementation-relevant research.
stop_when: Two or more strong sources support the same practical conclusion
New results are mostly duplicates or weaker restatements
The next code change is already clear
The bottleneck is engineering rather than missing knowledge

pending_tasks.description: CLOSED in SESSION-147. The SESSION-146 blackbox surfaced 'No knowledge bus injected — using heuristic fallback only' every time Director Studio was invoked: although DirectorIntentParser had long supported an external knowledge_bus parameter, both the interactive entrypoint (mathart/cli_wizard.py::_run_director_studio) and the non-interactive entrypoint (mathart/workspace/mode_dispatcher.py::DirectorStudioStrategy.execute) were instantiating the parser without one. pending_tasks.title: Rich topology-aware level semantics
pending_tasks.description: SESSION-109 CLOSES P1-ARCH-6 by landing a tensor-based topology substrate that lifts WFC tile grids into strongly-typed scene prims. New module mathart/level/topology_extractor.py implements a 5-stage pipeline (data-oriented mask building, SciPy 2D convolutional kernel pattern matching for floor/ceiling/wall/convex/concave/slope detection, vectorized surface-normal field, scipy.ndimage connected-component labelling for traversal lanes, and frozen-dataclass aggregation). New module mathart/level/topology_types.py pins the LEVEL_TOPOLOGY contract via three frozen dataclasses (SemanticAnchor, TraversalLane, TopologyTensors) plus a closed vocabulary of anchor types (floor_top/ceiling/wall_left/wall_right/corner_convex/corner_concave/slope) and surface kinds (floor/platform/slope) that reject any rogue strings at construction time. New plugin mathart/core/level_topology_backend.py self-registers via @register_backend(BackendType.LEVEL_TOPOLOGY), accepts the grid via inline ndarray, upstream WFC ArtifactManifest, or a tilemap_json path, and emits a typed ArtifactManifest of family LEVEL_TOPOLOGY with two outputs (topology_json + tensors_npz) and seven CI-required metadata keys. ArtifactFamily.LEVEL_TOPOLOGY + matching FAMILY_SCHEMAS / required_metadata_keys entries pin the schema in artifact_schema.py, and BackendType.LEVEL_TOPOLOGY (with aliases topology_extractor / level_topology_extractor / recast_topology / dual_grid_topology) closes the registry-side contract. 17/17 dedicated tests pass: 5 frozen-contract immutability/vocabulary tests, 7 algorithmic tests (room walls, free-standing convex corners, platform lanes, 256x256 wall-time budget, empty/all-solid degeneracy, unit-or-zero normals), 4 backend integration tests via MicrokernelPipelineBridge, and a 16-thread/32-batch/200x200 ThreadPoolExecutor pressure test that defends the anti-OOM red line by holding wall-time well under the 30s budget. Red lines respected: anti-OOM (no Python row/col loop over the grid; all heavy lifting is SciPy convolve2d / ndimage labelling), anti-hardcoded-tiles (data-oriented is_solid / is_empty masks driven by override-able tile-id sets, never tile names), anti-data-silo (frozen dataclasses + ArtifactManifest schema validation as the only cross-stage interface).
pending_tasks.validation: 17 PASS / 0 FAIL via pytest tests/test_level_topology.py (incl. 16-thread parallel pressure test) + level_topology backend passes the project-wide CI guard tests/test_ci_backend_schemas.py without any new failures.

The accompanying ReactionDiffusionBackend (mathart/core/reaction_diffusion_backend.py) self-registers via @register_backend, validates its own configuration, and emits a strongly-typed MATERIAL_BUNDLE ArtifactManifest with albedo/normal/height/mask PBR channels plus a raw fields.npz provenance archive. Hot path is 100% vectorized via scipy.ndimage.convolve(mode='wrap') — zero Python per-pixel loops, CFL-clamped dt, and np.clip-guarded bounds keep U,V in [0,1] indefinitely. Derived normal maps are unit-length within float32 precision and textures tile seamlessly under periodic boundaries. Advection hook (advection_field, advection_scheme) is reserved on GrayScottSolverConfig to cleanly absorb P1-RESEARCH-30C thermodynamic coupling in a future session. 43/43 targeted white-box tests PASS (tests/test_reaction_diffusion.py) covering AST loop guards, stencil math, CFL stability, seamless wrap, PBR unit-length normals, preset library, encoders, backend registration, and end-to-end manifest contract.

pending_tasks.title: Drive fluid VFX directly from UMR root velocity and weapon trajectories

(1) UMRKinematicImpulseAdapter — reads UnifiedMotionClip, extracts root velocity via native UMR fields or central finite difference, tracks weapon_tip end-effector positions/velocities from frame metadata, emits strongly-typed VectorFieldImpulse contracts with start/end segment, velocity_vector, Gaussian radius, and density. (3) CFL Soft Tanh Velocity Clamping — soft_tanh_clamp() limits injection magnitude to CFL-safe v_max = cfl_factor * dx / dt, preventing advection blow-up and NaN propagation (Anti-Energy-Explosion Guard). (4) FluidMomentumController — closed-loop orchestrator wiring UMR extraction → tensor splatting → direct grid field injection (u_prev/v_prev/density_prev) with obstacle constraint enforcement; auto-selects VFX config from clip state; supports dynamic obstacle masks (P1-VFX-1A compatibility). (5) MomentumVFXMetrics + evaluate_momentum_simulation — three-layer evolution bridge with NaN detection, field continuity scoring, and injection efficiency tracking. (6) 35/35 white-box regression tests across 9 test groups covering anti-dotted-line, anti-scalar-loop, CFL guard, UMR extraction, splatter geometry, closed-loop controller, evolution metrics, backward compatibility, and stress conditions. Research grounded in GPU Gems 3 Ch. 30 (Crane/Llamas/Tariq — Gaussian velocity splatting, free-slip BC), Continuous Trajectory Splatting (line-segment distance field), Naughty Dog/Sucker Punch animation-driven VFX (root/effector kinetic energy inheritance), and CFL stability condition (Courant-Friedrichs-Lewy).

completed_tasks.title: WFC locked-tile absolute survival and explicit conflict closure
completed_tasks.result: Refactored the base WFC propagation kernel into queue-driven domain restriction with read-only locked cells (`is_locked`). `_force_bottom_ground()` now locks cells through `_lock_cell()`, locked seeds propagate constraints outward from initialization via `_propagate_locked_cells()`, and any propagation that would shrink a locked domain raises `WFCConstraintConflictError` instead of silently overwriting ground truth. `ConstraintAwareWFC.generate()` now records `conflict_count` / `last_error` and re-raises the last contradiction instead of degrading to the old unconstrained fallback. Added deterministic adversarial tests for outward lock radiation, non-locked-to-locked overwrite conflict, and incompatible locked-tile dead-end with value-level state preservation. 46/46 targeted WFC + tilemap exporter regressions PASS.

architecture_notes.decision: Large commercial batch generation must route through PDG v2 fan-out and mark orthographic_pixel_render / anti_flicker_render as requires_gpu so gpu_slots remains the sole authority for VRAM admission control.
architecture_notes.implication: CPU-heavy genotype, motion, shell, and ribbon lanes can scale to 16 worker threads, while 12GB-class GPUs remain protected from batch-induced OOM during orthographic guide baking or ComfyUI sequence rendering.

architecture_notes.decision: The PDG-v2 mass factory must pass a concrete Mesh3D object into orthographic_pixel_render and must treat archive copying as part of the batch contract rather than an optional convenience.
architecture_notes.implication: Future batch-audit work can verify that orthographic guide maps were baked from the assembled character+equipment mesh, while operators can inspect a single per-character archive tree for Unity, Spine preview, orthographic guides, and skipped/live anti-flicker evidence without hunting through backend-private directories.

architecture_notes.decision: OrthographicPixelRenderBackend enforces a Fail-Fast Mesh3D Consumption Contract: missing, None, or geometrically empty mesh triggers PipelineContractError immediately. Zero fallback sphere generation. ArchiveDeliveryBackend is an independent registry plugin that collects all upstream artifacts into centralized archive/ directories. RNG spawn_digest is injected into every ArtifactManifest metadata dict.
architecture_notes.implication: The generator_invariant stagnation disaster (22,422 identical iterations from fallback sphere rendering) is permanently eliminated at the type-system level. Future ComfyUI cross-machine reproducibility work can trust that RNG digests in manifests are genuine and that archive/ directories contain real character geometry renders, not demo sphere placeholders. The ArchiveDeliveryBackend can be extended to support S3/GCS upload, content-addressable deduplication, and multi-machine batch aggregation without modifying any trunk code.

architecture_notes.decision: SESSION-128 establishes the deterministic foundation (RNG digest chain, fail-fast mesh contract, centralized archive) required before tackling ComfyUI cross-machine rendering reproducibility and GPU differentiable rendering acceleration.
architecture_notes.implication: Next steps for ComfyUI reproducibility: (1) Pin ComfyUI workflow JSON with deterministic node_id assignment and fixed seed injection per rng_spawn_digest. (2) Add GPU device fingerprint (CUDA version, driver, GPU model) to ArtifactManifest metadata for cross-machine comparison. (3) Implement frame-level pHash/SSIM regression between CPU dry-run and GPU live-run to quantify rendering divergence. (4) For GPU differentiable rendering: nvdiffrast or Mitsuba 3 can replace the NumPy software rasterizer while preserving the same Mesh3D consumption contract and ArtifactManifest output schema.

architecture_notes.decision: All animation export paths must enforce a strict bone topology contract: every bone name referenced in animation tracks must exist in the setup skeleton bone array. Quadruped NSM semantic limb names (front_left, hind_right, etc.) must be mapped to structural bone names (fl_upper, hr_upper, etc.) before Spine JSON export. Post-export validation raises PipelineContractError for any orphan animation track.
architecture_notes.implication: The 38-frame static quadruped animation disaster is permanently eliminated. Future gait types (hexapod, centipede, custom) must add their own NSM-to-bone mapping dictionaries and pass the same topology validation. The UE5 Retargeting discipline ensures that any downstream engine (Unity, Godot, Spine Runtime) will never encounter undefined bone references.

architecture_notes.decision: AntiFlickerRenderBackend now enforces a Fail-Fast dimension contract: missing width/height triggers PipelineContractError(missing_render_dimensions), dimensions < 64 triggers PipelineContractError(render_dimensions_too_small). When external source_frames/normal_maps/depth_maps are provided, the backend bypasses internal idle baking and inherits resolution from the guide sequence.
architecture_notes.implication: The 32x16 abstract color block disaster is permanently eliminated. The factory now passes guide-inherited resolution to the backend, ensuring the AI render output matches the orthographic render resolution. Future quality gate work can trust that all frames in the pipeline share a consistent resolution chain.

architecture_notes.decision: The factory's _image_sequence_from_render_manifest function now generates deterministic per-frame micro-variation (sub-pixel affine jitter + brightness perturbation seeded by frame_index and channel_hash) instead of replicating a single static image. This preserves SparseCtrl/AnimateDiff temporal attention signal while maintaining Bazel-level reproducibility.
architecture_notes.implication: Next steps for the Quality Gate system: (1) Add per-frame pHash computation to _build_guide_sequence output for intra-sequence uniqueness verification. (2) Implement optical flow extraction (Farneback or RAFT) between consecutive guide frames to quantify temporal coherence. (3) Build a QualityGateBackend that compares AI render output pHash/SSIM against guide input to detect temporal collapse. (4) Integrate FrameSequenceExporter (already exists in mathart/animation/frame_sequence_exporter.py) as the canonical sequence materialization path, replacing the current in-memory-only guide generation.

architecture_notes.decision: Factory guide sequence pipeline completely rewritten from single-image replication to true bone-driven per-frame rendering. _bake_true_motion_guide_sequence() reconstructs CharacterGenotype from serialized JSON, builds animation_func from Clip2D bone transforms with frame-accurate interpolation, and renders genuine per-frame guide sequences (source/normal/depth/mask) through the industrial renderer or character_renderer fallback. OOM-safe chunked rendering (CHUNK_SIZE=8) with explicit numpy array deletion. PDG topology corrected: ai_render_stage now depends on motion2d_export_stage.
architecture_notes.implication: The AI rendering backend now receives guide sequences with real geometric variation from actual skeletal animation, enabling AnimateDiff/SparseCtrl temporal attention to produce coherent motion. The Clip2D-to-animation_func bridge is the canonical path for all future guide sequence generation. The industrial_renderer/character_renderer fallback chain ensures graceful degradation without sacrificing temporal variance.

architecture_notes.decision: validate_temporal_variance() in anti_flicker_runtime.py computes inter-frame MSE for consecutive pairs. Sequences where fewer than 50% of pairs exceed MSE threshold 1.0 are rejected with PipelineContractError(temporal_variance_below_threshold). This catches both static forgery (0 MSE) and SESSION-129 micro-jitter forgery (MSE < 0.1). compute_frame_hashes() provides SHA-256 per-frame pixel audit trail for anti-forgery verification.
architecture_notes.implication: The circuit breaker is the first line of defense in the Quality Gate system. It operates at the memory-interchange boundary between guide sequence generation and ComfyUI payload assembly, catching forgeries in microseconds before they waste GPU time. The TemporalVarianceReport dataclass provides structured diagnostics for automated CI and manual debugging. The MSE threshold (1.0) and distinct ratio (0.5) are configurable parameters that can be tuned per-niche or per-style.

architecture_notes.decision: With true motion guide sequences and the temporal variance circuit breaker in place, the architecture is now ready for the full Quality Gate system based on multi-frame optical flow (Motion Vectors) and SSIM temporal consistency.
architecture_notes.implication: Next steps for the Quality Gate system: (1) Add optical flow extraction (Farneback or RAFT) between consecutive AI render output frames to compute motion vector fields. (2) Implement SSIM temporal consistency scoring: compare AI render output frame-pairs against guide input frame-pairs to detect temporal collapse or hallucination. (3) Build QualityGateBackend as a registry plugin that runs post-AI-render and produces a QUALITY_GATE_REPORT ArtifactManifest with per-frame scores. (4) Implement auto-fuse mechanism: if SSIM drops below threshold for >3 consecutive frames, trigger re-render with adjusted ControlNet strength. (5) Integrate FrameSequenceExporter as the canonical disk-materialization path for guide sequences, enabling ComfyUI VHS_LoadImagesPath directory-based loading.

architecture_notes.decision: New mathart/quality/temporal_quality_gate.py implements a three-state circuit breaker (CLOSED/OPEN/HALF_OPEN) per Martin Fowler's pattern. Sliding-window warp-SSIM computation uses occlusion-aware GT motion vectors from motion_vector_baker. Min-SSIM fuse (NOT mean-SSIM) ensures a single catastrophic frame triggers rejection. OOM-safe O(1) memory per pair via sliding window — only two frames and one MV field are resident at any time.
architecture_notes.implication: The circuit breaker is the authoritative temporal quality defense layer. It sits between AI render output and artifact persistence, ensuring defective sequences never reach disk. The three-state machine enables graceful recovery: after cooldown_seconds (60s default), the breaker enters HALF_OPEN and allows a single probe evaluation. If the probe passes, the breaker returns to CLOSED. This prevents permanent lockout from transient ComfyUI instability.

architecture_notes.decision: TemporalConsistencyMetrics extended with min_warp_ssim, per_pair_warp_ssim, and worst_frame_pair_index. evaluate_temporal_consistency() now computes Min-SSIM as a secondary pass criterion (threshold 0.5). compute_temporal_fitness_bonus() adds a catastrophic frame penalty: when min_warp_ssim < 0.5, a penalty of up to -0.25 is applied to the evolution fitness landscape via configurable min_warp_ssim_penalty_lambda.
architecture_notes.implication: The evolution engine now has a direct negative feedback loop from temporal quality to genotype fitness. Characters whose morphology or gait produces temporally incoherent AI renders will be penalized in the fitness landscape, steering evolution toward motion patterns that produce stable, coherent video output. This closes the loop from SESSION-130's true motion guide to SESSION-131's quality-driven evolution.

architecture_notes.decision: With the temporal quality gate and min_warp_ssim fitness penalty in place, the architecture is now ready for MAP-Elites multi-objective evolution that simultaneously optimizes motion coherence and character style diversity.
architecture_notes.implication: Next steps for MAP-Elites: (1) Define behavior descriptor space — 2D grid with axes [temporal_coherence_score, style_diversity_score] where temporal_coherence comes from TemporalQualityGate and style_diversity from visual_fitness pHash distance to archive centroid. (2) Implement MAP-Elites archive — a grid of elite cells, each storing the best genotype for its behavior descriptor bin. (3) Modify evolution loop — after fitness evaluation, compute behavior descriptors and attempt to place the individual into the MAP-Elites archive (replace if fitness is higher). (4) Add curiosity-driven exploration bonus — genotypes that fill empty archive cells receive a one-time fitness bonus to encourage coverage of the behavior space. (5) Integrate TemporalQualityGate verdict as a hard constraint — genotypes that fail the circuit breaker are never placed in the archive regardless of other fitness scores.

architecture_notes.decision: All business-critical diagnostic payloads (radar JSON, launcher abort, proxy render failure, Truth Gateway arbitration, CLI wizard route selection) MUST be mirrored into the blackbox flight recorder via logger.info/warning calls. Terminal stdout is for user-facing UX only; the blackbox is the single source of truth for post-mortem analysis. cli.py must never use logging.basicConfig(force=True) as it destroys the blackbox file handler.
architecture_notes.implication: Post-mortem analysis of production failures, white-model crashes, and knowledge constraint violations no longer depends on terminal scrollback. The blackbox file (logs/mathart.log) now contains the complete audit trail. Future log platform integration (ELK/Loki) can ingest structured telemetry directly. The dual-track console=WARNING / file=DEBUG discipline ensures the wizard TUI remains clean while the blackbox captures everything.

architecture_notes.decision: psutil is a lightweight process-table utility required by PreflightRadar and MUST be in core dependencies. torch is a multi-GB heavy AI runtime and MUST NEVER appear in core dependencies; it is isolated to [project.optional-dependencies].gpu. All heavy optional imports (torch, gymnasium, numba, optuna) must use lazy-import guards at function scope, never at module top level.
architecture_notes.implication: CPU-only sandbox startup remains sub-second. Users who need GPU SDF evaluation or neural rendering explicitly opt in via pip install mathart[gpu]. The lazy-import discipline is enforced by SESSION-146 tests and can be extended to a CI-level AST scan in P1-SESSION-146-IMPORT-CONTRACT-AUDIT.

architecture_notes.decision: The ComfyUI discovery radar MUST cover all mainstream deployment patterns across Windows (including Portable distribution), macOS, and Linux. Static candidate paths expanded from 14 to 42. Relative-to-cwd probes expanded to include grandparent directories and Portable variants. Every filesystem probe and process-table walk step MUST emit DEBUG-level logs with [Radar/FS] and [Radar/PS] tags so that logs/mathart.log contains a complete search audit trail when ComfyUI is not found. Discovery-level summaries use INFO (found) and WARNING (not found) levels.
architecture_notes.implication: When a user reports comfyui_not_found, support engineers can request logs/mathart.log and immediately see every path the radar probed, every process it inspected, and exactly why each candidate was rejected. This eliminates the previous排障无门 problem. The DEBUG-level audit trail stays invisible on the terminal (console=WARNING guard) but is fully captured in the file handler. Future enhancements can add Windows Registry queries and PATH-based discovery as additional layers.
